marketState: neutral-to-bearish — unanimous bearish technicals and a bigger-than-expected storage build outweigh a fairly-valued, low-reliability seasonal picture
summary: Front-month NG is trading essentially at fair value ($2.838 vs $2.923 fair, inside the $2.42-$3.42 band) after a year that's run -16.7% YTD versus a -6.9% median, so there's no glaring mispricing to lean on. Near-term weather is hot (1-7D demand +20.5% vs 5y, near the top of its range) but that support fades by days 8-16 (-3.9% vs 5y), while all six technical timeframes (5m through 1w) are aligned bearish with 50<200 EMA crosses, even as RSI in the low-to-mid 30s and a sub-lower-Bollinger 4h close hint at short-term oversold conditions. Storage printed a bigger-than-expected build (+22 Bcf surprise, +180 Bcf vs 5y), reinforcing the bearish supply backdrop, and managed money is net short but only at the 35th percentile of its 5-year range — bearish positioning, not an extreme ripe for a short-covering squeeze. Seasonality gives only a weak bearish lean (38% odds of higher prices in 3-4 weeks, typical 4-week move -1.4%) but is explicitly flagged Low reliability this week (59pp 10-90 spread), so it shouldn't be weighted heavily. Net: a soft bearish tilt for the next 1-10 days on trend/storage, but no clean multi-week edge given the fairly-valued price and the large winter contango (Dec $3.71, Jan $4.14) already baked into the curve.

longTerm.rationale: Storage surplus (+180 Bcf vs 5y) and a bearish surprise argue for continued softness into shoulder season, but front price already sits right at fair value (not stretched cheap or rich), COT shorts aren't at an extreme, and the futures curve already prices in a large winter premium (Dec/Jan $3.7-4.1) that a sustained short would have to fight through the roll. Seasonality's own 38% probHigher lean is explicitly tagged Low reliability, so it shouldn't drive a trade on its own.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for either (a) a pullback toward the fair-value band's lower edge (~$2.45-2.55) to buy ahead of the Nov-Jan seasonal ramp already in the curve, or (b) clearer bearish confirmation — storage surplus widening further or managed-money COT positioning pushing toward extreme net-short (currently just 35th percentile) — before selling. The current mix of bearish storage/COT vs a fairly-valued, contango-supported curve doesn't offer a clean edge yet.

shortTerm.entry: $2.83 - $2.88 (sell into any bounce; market entry near $2.84 acceptable)
shortTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; consider trimming half the position at TP1
shortTerm.rationale: All six technical timeframes (5m-1w) show a bearish trend with 50<200 EMA crosses, and the 4h chart closed below its lower Bollinger Band; the larger-than-forecast storage build (+22 Bcf surprise) reinforces the bearish tape. Front price is fair-valued rather than fundamentally oversold, so trend continuation is the higher-probability path over the next 1-10 days, though RSI in the low-30s leaves room for a bounce first.
shortTerm.invalidation: A close back above ~$2.95-3.00 (near the plus7D fair value), or a sharp near-term acceleration in the already-hot 1-7 day cooling demand feeding into spot tightness, would invalidate the short and argue for covering.

Storage surplus widening (+180 Bcf vs 5y) with a bearish +22 Bcf surprise vs forecast
Near-term weather demand hot (+20.5% vs 5y, rangePos 0.83) but fading by day 8-16 (-3.9% vs 5y)
Front price sitting right at fair value ($2.838 vs $2.923 fair) — no extreme mispricing to trade
Managed-money net short but only 35th percentile of 5y range — bearish tilt, not an extreme
risks: Hot 1-7 day weather could spark a short-covering bounce against the prevailing bearish trend
